Tony
Jennifer
Fabar
Kristy
Long time no see, Detective.
Hello.
The view from here is fantastic, but don’t you think my place is prettier?
Both look equally wonderful to me.
Oh, what a straight-A student answer.

The sun is setting, and the scenery dyed in crimson is beautiful. Leaving the curtains open was a good call.
In the spacious room, small groups of people have formed. Compared to the party before the crank in, this place is terribly sparse.
He raises his glass to his lips with a face as if nothing happened, chatting with the customer next to him.
At least he's maintaining a calm demeanor on the surface. I wonder if this is a trait of city dwellers.
He looks a bit thinner and older compared to the first time we met at the director’s party.
He doesn’t change his expression at all when he sees me. His former timid attitude seems to have completely faded away.
Even seeing Meryl with a knife, he didn't change his expression. Not even a little.
Why is that?
It's as if he had predicted everything Meryl would do; his calm attitude hasn't changed at all.
Don't you think it's pretty sparse compared to Boston?
That's true. It's sad that some people haven't compared it to before.
Absolutely. Let me put this out there from the start, let's skip the awkward topics today.
So what about afterwards? Did you cut ties with Gordon?
It's a party today, right? I don't want to hear any unnecessary talk.
You don't seem anxious like before. It looks like you've managed to get through it somehow.
Can't you hear? I'm saying I don't want to hear that kind of talk.
With this, it’s goodbye to the bad luck of filming. Do you think anyone was the jinx?
I thought it was a good performance, but that was a serious act, huh.
Is that about Meryl's interruption?
That's right. Nothing else really happened.
I thought he would do something cool.
He’s not that thoughtful of a guy.
You're not surprised at all, huh? So you’re used to this kind of thing.
Is that a sarcastic remark about me having endless arguments with women while being brandished with knives?
Not really.
I don't mean anything by it.
Women are scary, huh? Who knows what they might do.
Today's...
...feels a bit low energy. Maybe the movie getting shelved is hitting hard?
You seem a bit tired, huh. Is it really because of the movie?
If it’s not about the movie, it might be about... women's troubles.
Well, he seems to have a lot of energy for his age. It's not surprising if it’s about a woman.
By the way,
what do you think happened between him and Meryl?
Aren't they still dating?
It's hard to tell from how things looked earlier.
The reason Meryl is upset might be due to her conflict with Fabar.
Absolutely.
That old man is something else.
Hmph, for someone who was supposedly under house arrest, he looks strangely healthy.
I don't think the Nets Corporation would confine their players in a way that makes them sluggish.
That's true, but there’s just something about him I don't like.
Was he really under house arrest? Or was it all an act to create an alibi?
Goodness, it was like watching a play, wasn't it?
Such a thing.
Aren't you shocked that Meryl has turned out this way?
Feel shock? I'm sure nothing as shocking as my life exists in this world.
Gai isn't someone to underestimate either, what did he do to Meryl?
Wow, she seems to have an abundance of energy today. She's practically a female version of Gai.
I'm sure Gai came back, repaired their relationship, and now she's happy.
I see.
But that beaming smile is a far cry from an intellectual impression. Not my type.
Why are women like that?
Making a big fuss over just one man.
It was amazing. She jumped right in between Gai and Meryl. I felt a maternal instinct from her protecting Gai.
She must have acted on impulse. For her, getting hurt probably didn't matter one bit.
Are you feeling jealous? As her ex.
You shouldn't push yourself too hard, you know. Enduring isn't good.
Seems like today's princess is in a foul mood. Even without that dog-and-cat Meryl around, she looks down in the dumps.
It does seem that way.
Is it okay for you to make that face? I thought you were supposed to be the princess's knight.
The next movie is
probably set in stone.
I wonder, that gloomy face looks like she's more depressed watching Meryl's disgrace.
It's sinking along with her.
Do you know why those two are down?
I have no idea. Maybe they have some common worries between them.
Do those two feel guilty about what happened to Meryl?
I never thought she would turn out to be such an embarrassment. Life, you never know where the fall begins.
Poor her.
Isn't 'falling in life' a bit over the top?
It's not. She walked down 5th Avenue looking like that. The press won't miss that, right?
From now on, her former status will turn to dust, and she'll either end up as a laughingstock or be blacklisted.
No way.
There's no way that could happen.
Nothing's impossible in this world, you know.
It's really unfortunate. A talented actress has gone to waste.
Whether Guy is the serial killer or not, now they say Cindy's incident is also the work of a serial killer.
These days, the media links everything to serial murder.
That's just how things are in any era.
Just so you know, I have nothing to do with it.
Who is this Ariana that Meryl was talking about?
Uh, I don’t know.
Who do you think it is?
I don’t understand why he would attack Guy. What on earth happened?
Ugh, New York is such a terrible city.
That's a rare key; its shape is artistic.
Isn't this yours?
Unfortunately, I've never seen it before.
No matter how many times I see it, I don't know what it is.
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
